ground_truth.title: May 2025 Bootcamp News Roundup
ground_truth.author: Liz Eggleston
ground_truth.main_content:
The bootcamp industry continues to evolve this May, with new campuses opening around the world, innovative partnerships forming between bootcamps and colleges, and AI-focused programs gaining traction at universities and training providers alike. In this May 2025 news roundup, we highlight news about big moves from Le Wagon, Resilient Coders, and Zip Code Wilmington, track the rise of AI bootcamps, and share five new schools added to the Course Report directory.

NEW CAMPUSES & SCHOOLS IN THE NEWS: Le Wagon is opening a new classroom in Bangalore – its first campus in India, with the first cohort starting July 7th. Resilient Coders has been training underrepresented Black and brown talent in software engineering since 2014, offering students an intensive, stipended, 18-week full-stack engineering course, an AI/ML training program with immersive agile development cycles, professional development, mentorship and ongoing career placement support.

DEGREES VS BOOTCAMPS: According to HRDive, 1 in 4 employers say they'll eliminate degree requirements by year's end. Companies that recently removed degree requirements reported a surge in applications and a more diverse applicant pool. Zip Code Wilmington has announced a new partnership with Goldey-Beacom College that will allow graduates of its coding bootcamps to earn up to 12 college credits through the Credit for Prior Learning initiative.

AI BOOTCAMPS ON THE RISE: Stellenbosch University and HyperionDev are launching a Generative Artificial Intelligence mini-bootcamp covering fundamental AI topics from the math behind machine learning to deep learning models. Washburn University is launching a new Artificial Intelligence Literacy and Application Certificate in Fall 2025. University of Washington's Allen School will offer a new part-time evening program called Graduate Certificate in Modern AI Methods.

5 NEW SCHOOLS ADDED TO COURSE REPORT: Charis Intelligence, Tuwaiq Academy, Red Alpha Cybersecurity, Skiller Academy, and Boolean Academia Chile have been added to the Course Report directory.

without: Course Report strives to create the most trust-worthy content about coding bootcamps.
